meta.title: Bible
meta.note: Written for ~age 12. Themes drawn from common teen verse lists (Joshua 1:9, Philippians 4:13, 1 Peter 5:7, Psalm 139:14, etc.).

items.prompt: A shepherd boy fought a giant with a sling. Who was he?
items.takeaway: You don't need to be the biggest kid. God helps brave hearts.

items.prompt: Finish it: "Be strong and courageous… God will be with you ___ you go."
items.takeaway: Feeling nervous before a game, test, or new school? God goes with you.

items.prompt: Finish it: "I can do all things through ___ who strengthens me."
items.takeaway: Hard days happen. Jesus gives strength when you feel done.

items.prompt: Jesus stopped a huge storm with His words. True or false?
items.takeaway: When life feels wild, Jesus is still in charge.

items.prompt: Who got thrown into a lions' den for praying—and God kept him safe?
items.takeaway: Doing the right thing can be hard. God sees you.

items.prompt: True or false: You're too young to matter to God.
items.verse: 1 Timothy 4:12 — Don't let anyone look down on you because you are young.
items.takeaway: At 12 you can already be kind, honest, and brave.

items.prompt: "If God is for us, who can be against us?" Where is that?
items.takeaway: Mean comments don't get the final say. God is on your side.

items.prompt: When you don't know what to do, Proverbs says: Trust in the Lord… and don't lean only on your own ___.
items.takeaway: Ask God for help. You don't have to figure everything out alone.

items.prompt: Psalm 139 says you are "fearfully and ___ made."
items.takeaway: You are not a mistake. God made you on purpose.

items.prompt: Psalm 23 says even in a dark valley, "I will fear no ___."
items.takeaway: Scary moments happen. God walks with you through them.

items.prompt: Joseph's brothers sold him. Years later, Joseph helped save many people. Did God waste Joseph's hard years?
items.verse: Genesis 50:20 — You meant harm, but God meant it for good.
items.takeaway: Bad days aren't the end of your story.
items.answer: No — God used them for good

items.prompt: God told Gideon to fight with only 300 men. Why so few?
items.takeaway: God likes to show that He is strong—even when we feel small.
items.answer: So people would know God won

items.prompt: Ephesians says: "In your anger do not ___."
items.takeaway: It's okay to feel mad. It's not okay to hurt people or stay bitter.

items.prompt: Proverbs says the tongue has the power of life and ___.
items.takeaway: Words can build a friend up—or wreck their day. Choose life.

items.prompt: God has not given us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and a ___ mind.
items.takeaway: Fear doesn't get to run your life.

items.prompt: John 3:16 — God loved the world so much that He gave His one and only ___.
items.takeaway: Jesus came because God loves you—for real.

items.prompt: "Bad company corrupts good character." What does that mean for friends?
items.takeaway: Friends shape you. Pick people who help you be better, not worse.
items.answer: Friends who do wrong can pull you into wrong

items.prompt: Jesus said, "I am with you ___."
items.takeaway: You're never alone—not at school, not at night, not when you're scared.

items.prompt: If you know the right thing and won't do it, James calls that ___.
items.takeaway: Don't just know what's right—do it.

items.prompt: Those who hope in the Lord will renew their ___.
items.takeaway: Tired? Ask God for fresh strength.

items.prompt: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego got thrown in a fire for not bowing to a statue. Did they burn up?
items.takeaway: They stood for God even when it was scary—and God was with them.

items.prompt: The golden rule: Treat others the way you want them to ___ you.
items.takeaway: Before you tease or ignore someone, ask: Would I want that?

items.prompt: Jeremiah said he was too young. God said: Don't say that—I am with you. True or false?
items.takeaway: God can use kids. Age is not a no.

items.prompt: Hebrews says to run your race with your eyes fixed on ___.
items.takeaway: When you want to quit, look to Jesus and keep going.

items.prompt: 1 Peter 5:7 — Cast all your ___ on Him, because He cares for you.
items.takeaway: Worried about school, friends, or home? Tell God. He cares.

items.prompt: "Do not fear, for I am with you… I will strengthen you." Which book?
items.takeaway: God doesn't just say "don't be scared"—He helps you.

items.prompt: Philippians says: Don't worry about anything. Instead, ___ about everything.
items.takeaway: Turn worry into prayer. God gives peace.

items.prompt: Jesus said the most important commands are: Love God, and love your ___ as yourself.
items.takeaway: Faith isn't just church talk—it's how you treat people.

items.prompt: Noah built a huge boat when God told him to. Did people think that was weird?
items.takeaway: Obeying God matters more than looking cool.

items.prompt: "A friend loves at all times." What kind of friend is that?
items.takeaway: Real friends stick around on bad days, not just fun days.

items.prompt: "The Lord is my light and my salvation—whom shall I ___?"
items.takeaway: God is bigger than what scares you.

items.prompt: Colossians says: Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart—as working for ___.
items.takeaway: Homework, chores, practice—do them for God, not just for grades or parents.

items.prompt: Jesus said: "Come to me, all you who are weary… and I will give you ___."
items.takeaway: Burned out? Jesus invites you to rest with Him.

items.prompt: Ephesians says we are God's ___, created to do good things.
items.takeaway: You're God's handiwork—not leftover scrap.

items.prompt: "Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths." What should your words do instead?
items.takeaway: Build people up. Don't trash-talk.

items.prompt: Moses was scared to speak for God. Did God still use him?
items.takeaway: Feeling scared doesn't mean God can't use you.

items.prompt: "Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid… God goes with you." That's from Deuteronomy. True or false?
items.takeaway: God won't leave you hanging.

items.prompt: Jesus said: Don't worry about tomorrow. Why?
items.takeaway: Handle today's stuff with God. Don't carry tomorrow's stress early.
items.answer: Tomorrow has enough trouble of its own

items.prompt: "This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it." Can a normal school day count?
items.takeaway: Yes—ordinary days are still gifts from God.

items.prompt: Jesus said: "I am the way and the truth and the ___."
items.takeaway: Following Jesus is the path that leads to real life.
